calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF DEATH
Off Calendar Oct-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Deborah Fox for (petitioner) Dianne Hayden.

The Court stated that it needs a clearer statement that Mr. Hayden was actually on the boat and not just presumed, that it has reviewed the incident reports and that there is nothing to say that he was on board. Ms. Fox stated that under the circumstances it is clear that he is missing, that there was no question by the Sheriff that he was on the boat and that they could get a declaration by a survivor that he was on the boat. The Court stated that it does not have a Sheriff's report indicating that he was on the boat. Ms. Fox stated that there are no grounds for the courts concern. The Court stated that it is being asked to clear that he died, that it has a report that a search was made and that if anyone was left in the water they would have died but that there is nothing to say that he was actually on the boat. The Court further stated that it does not wish to put the petitioners through any additional grief. Ms. Fox stated that everything the court needs is before it. The Court stated that it will take the petition to establish fact of death under submission and that if counsel can, submit something that stated there is a reason they were searching for him.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
